# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.
# Category: container_security

# Use the official Node.js LTS image as the base image
FROM node:lts

# Set working directory in the container
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json to the container
COPY package*.json ./

# Install dependencies
RUN npm install

# Copy the rest of the application code to the container
COPY . .

# Expose the port the app runs on
EXPOSE 3000

# Start the application
CMD ["node", "app.js"]
